originalTitle: Iran's threats against this Red Sea choke point are a big vulnerability for the oil market
summary: Oil prices would spike if Iran's Houthi allies started attacking ships passing through the Bab el-Mandeb Strait.
content:
President Donald Trump faces the risk that Iran will close the Bab el-Mandeb Strait if the conflict in the Middle East escalates, a scenario that would slash oil supplies to an already deeply disrupted market.

The Bab el-Mandeb is one of the world's key trade choke points, connecting the Red Sea to the Gulf of Aden and the Arabian Sea. It has acted as a crucial relief valve for the oil market as exports through the Strait of Hormuz have plunged due to Iranian attacks on tanker and cargo ships.

Saudi Arabia surged oil flows through its East-West Pipeline after the Hormuz closed, redirecting millions of barrels per day to the Red Sea. Those barrels are transiting the Bab el-Mandeb to Asia, which has helped offset some of the lost supply to key economies like Japan and South Korea.

Oil and product exports through the Bab el-Mandeb nearly doubled to 7.2 million barrels per day in April compared with 3.9 million bpd in February before the U.S. and Israel attacked Iran, according to data provided by Kpler.

Iran's Revolutionary Guard threatened Monday to close the Bab el-Mandeb if Israel did not halt strikes in Gaza and Lebanon, according to the Iranian state news agency Tasnim. Tehran has insisted that any peace deal with the U.S. must include the withdrawal of Israel from Lebanon.

Iran closing the Bab el-Mandeb would cut off the Saudi barrels that are heading to Asia, said Matt Smith, director of commodity research at Kpler.

"That would be a step up in terms of escalation and in terms of market impact," Smith said. The flow of oil through the Red Sea is one of the factors that has kept crude prices from surging higher, he said.

U.S. crude oil prices spiked 8% at the session high Monday after Iran's threat against Bab el-Mandeb. Prices pulled back after Israel and Lebanon agreed Wednesday to implement a ceasefire, but it is far from certain the truce will actually take effect.

Iran's Lebanese ally Hezbollah, which acts independently from the government in Beirut, rejected the ceasefire deal Thursday. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu told CNBC on Wednesday that "we have to disarm Hezbollah and we have to demilitarize Lebanon."

The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran remains fragile. Washington and Tehran exchanged fire in and around the Strait of Hormuz earlier this week. If the U.S. escalates military action, Iran's natural response would be to target Bab el-Mandeb, Smith said.

Iran's Houthi allies in Yemen have largely stayed out of the war so far. The Houthis attacked commercial ships in the Red Sea from 2023 through 2025 in retaliation for Israel's war in Gaza. Traffic through the Bab el-Mandeb plunged and has never fully recovered.

The Trump administration waged a 52-day air war against the Houthis that ended in May 2025 with a ceasefire. The U.S. stopped its strikes in exchange for the militants ending their attacks on U.S.-flagged ships in the Red Sea.

The Houthis may be waiting to enter the current conflict until the Iranian leadership decides its advantageous to open another front, said Jack Kennedy, head of Middle East country risk at S&P Global Market Intelligence.

The Houthis wouldn't have to do much to cut traffic through the Bab el-Mandeb, Smith said.

"They wouldn't have to fire at every single tanker that was passing through there," the Kpler analyst said. "Some specific targets would be enough to start deterring the passage through there."
chineseTitle: 伊朗对红海咽喉要道的威胁是石油市场的一大弱点
chineseSummary: 如果伊朗的胡塞盟友开始攻击通过曼德海峡的船只，油价将会飙升。
chineseContent:
如果中东冲突升级，唐纳德·特朗普总统面临着伊朗将关闭曼德海峡的风险，这种情况将大幅削减对本已严重混乱的市场的石油供应。

曼德海峡是世界主要贸易咽喉要道之一，连接红海、亚丁湾和阿拉伯海。由于伊朗袭击油轮和货船，霍尔木兹海峡的出口大幅下降，它成为石油市场的重要减压阀。

霍尔木兹海峡关闭后，沙特阿拉伯通过其东西管道输送的石油流量激增，每天将数百万桶石油重新输送到红海。这些石油正在通过曼德海峡运往亚洲，这有助于抵消日本和韩国等主要经济体的部分供应损失。

Kpler提供的数据显示，4月份通过曼德海峡的石油和产品出口量几乎翻了一番，达到每天720万桶，而美国和以色列袭击伊朗之前的2月份为每天390万桶。

据伊朗国家通讯社塔斯尼姆报道，伊朗革命卫队周一威胁称，如果以色列不停止对加沙和黎巴嫩的袭击，伊朗革命卫队将关闭曼德门。德黑兰坚称，与美国的任何和平协议都必须包括以色列从黎巴嫩撤军。

Kpler 大宗商品研究主管马特·史密斯表示，伊朗关闭曼德海峡将切断前往亚洲的沙特石油。

史密斯说：“就事态升级和市场影响而言，这将是一个进步。”他说，通过红海的石油流动是阻止原油价格飙升的因素之一。

在伊朗对曼德布发出威胁后，美国原油价格周一飙升 8%，创盘中高点。以色列和黎巴嫩周三同意实施停火后，价格回落，但目前还很难确定停火是否会真正生效。

伊朗的黎巴嫩盟友真主党独立于贝鲁特政府行事，周四拒绝了停火协议。以色列总理本杰明·内塔尼亚胡周三对 CNBC 表示，“我们必须解除真主党的武装，我们必须使黎巴嫩非军事化。”

美国和伊朗之间的停火仍然脆弱。华盛顿和德黑兰本周早些时候在霍尔木兹海峡及其周边地区交火。史密斯说，如果美国升级军事行动，伊朗的自然反应将是瞄准曼德海峡。

到目前为止，伊朗在也门的胡塞盟友基本上没有卷入战争。 2023年至2025年，胡塞武装袭击了红海商船，以报复以色列在加沙的战争。通过曼德海峡的交通量急剧下降，并且从未完全恢复。

特朗普政府对胡塞武装发动了为期 52 天的空战，并于 2025 年 5 月停火。美国停止袭击，以换取武装分子停止对红海悬挂美国国旗的船只的袭击。

标准普尔全球市场情报公司中东国家风险主管杰克·肯尼迪表示，胡塞武装可能会等待伊朗领导层认为开辟另一条战线有利，然后才加入当前的冲突。

史密斯说，胡塞武装不需要采取太多行动来切断曼德海峡的交通。

克普勒分析师表示：“他们不必向每一艘经过那里的油轮开火。” “一些具体目标就足以开始阻止通过那里的通道。”
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
